content.problemStatement:
You have two water jugs of capacity XXX and YYY litres. Both jugs are initially empty and you have an infinite water supply. The jugs don't have any markings to measure smaller quantities. Your task is to determine if it's possible to measure exactly ZZZ litres using these jugs.

You can perform the following operations:


Fill any of the jugs completely with water
Empty any of the jugs completely
Pour water from one jug into another until either:
If ZZZ litres is measurable, you must have exactly ZZZ litres in one or both jugs at the end.


Input Format

First line contains TTT - the number of test cases
For each test case:

First line contains three integers XXX, YYY, and ZZZ



XXX: Capacity of first jug 

YYY: Capacity of second jug 

ZZZ: Target volume to measure



Output Format

For each test case:

Print "YES" if it's possible to measure Z litres
Print "NO" if it's impossible

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1: X=4X = 4X=4, Y=3Y = 3Y=3, Z=2Z = 2Z=2

Fill jug 1 (4, 0)
Pour into jug 2 until full (1, 3)
Empty jug 2 (1, 0)
Pour jug 1 into jug 2 (0, 1)
Fill jug 1 (4, 1)
Pour into jug 2 until full (2, 3)
Now jug 1 has exactly 2 litres!

Test Case 2: X=3X = 3X=3, Y=5Y = 5Y=5, Z=4Z = 4Z=4

Fill jug 2 (0, 5)
Pour into jug 1 until full (3, 2)
Empty jug 1 (0, 2)
Pour jug 2 into jug 1 (2, 0)
Fill jug 2 (2, 5)
Pour into jug 1 until full (3, 4)
Now jug 2 has exactly 4 litres!
